content: We can adapt coordinate-wise normalization to stabilize intrinsic reward scaling from the ICM curiosity mechanism in autonomous agents, by normalizing each of the ICM's inverse and forward prediction error intrinsic reward streams independently instead of applying a global scaling to the combined intrinsic reward. This addresses the common 2025-era pain point of intrinsic reward vanishing or exploding as agents explore large, unstructured state spaces, where different state feature dimensions have wildly different scales of prediction error.
